subject: Re: WarpTools crashing on frame motion correction
body:
Hi Valerie,

I'm still learning, so I don't have a strong understanding of what each 
line of code does. However, I can give you an explanation to the best of my 
ability. Someone else reading may be able to give additional guidance or 
correct any missteps I made in explanation.

>From my understanding, in our HPC environment, data transfer/manipulation 
and login/computational nodes access the network differently. Computational 
nodes access the network through a proxy server and each module that 
requires network access must have the Lmod files individually modified to 
permit proxy access. This is done through a script called 
"enable_proxy.sh," which has to be called.

To call "enable_proxy.sh," my IT department made the changes copied below:

setenv("WARP_HOME",pathJoin(base,app,version))
source_sh("bash","$WARP_HOME/libexec/enable_proxy.sh")

The following scripts were also used to troubleshoot:

############ To check the content of " enable_proxy.sh ":

#!/bin/bash
proxyon >& /dev/null
export 
no_proxy=localhost,127.0.0.1,$(hostname),$(hostname).your.network.address

############ To check "proxyon:"

proxyon ()
{
    echo "[+] Enabling web proxy ...";
    export http_proxy=http://dtn${HOSTNUM}-e0:${SQUID_PORT};
    export https_proxy=http://dtn${HOSTNUM}-e0:${SQUID_PORT};
    export ftp_proxy=http://dtn${HOSTNUM}-e0:${SQUID_PORT};
    export RSYNC_PROXY=dtn${HOSTNUM}-e0:${SQUID_PORT}

I hope that this will be helpful in solving your issue!

Best,
Daniel

On Monday, August 12, 2024 at 12:36:04 PM UTC-4 Valerie Perez wrote:

> Hello Daniel,
>
> This may be a stretch, but did your IT department explain how they 
> configured Warp to access the proxy server? I am having the exact same 
> issue and haven't come to a solution.
>
> The issue happens both on our local workstation as well as when we try to 
> run WarpTools on our cluster. Any suggestions are greatly appreciated!
>
> Thank you,
> Valerie
>
> On Thursday, August 1, 2024 at 1:30:25 PM UTC-5 dxh...@gmail.com wrote:
>
>> Hi Alister,
>>
>> Thanks for the clarification. 
>> The scientific computing support staff also gave me some more information 
>> on why it failed: our computing nodes on which our warp was installed 
>> access the network through a proxy server. Warp had to be configured to 
>> enable access to the proxy server and properly function.
>>
>> Best,
>> Daniel
>>
>>
>>
>>
>>
>> On Saturday, July 27, 2024 at 2:30:57 AM UTC-4 alist...@gmail.com wrote:
>>
>>> Hi Daniel,
>>>
>>> Thanks for the update, just adding a small clarification. No access to 
>>> the external internet is required to run WarpTools. WarpTools communicates 
>>> with worker processes over a REST API. In standard usage this is all local 
>>> to the machine you’re running on.
>>>
>>> Cheers,
>>>
>>> Alister
>>>
>>>
>>> Sent from mobile - apologies for brevity
>>>
>>> On Jul 26, 2024, at 21:35, Daniel Cai <dxh...@gmail.com> wrote:
>>>
>>> ﻿Hi Alister,
>>>
>>>
>>> I got my IT department to check in on the installation and they 
>>> discovered that Warp was unable to connect to the internet the way it was 
>>> installed into our HPC environment. After modifying to give internet 
>>> access, I was able to successfully run the tutorial data.
>>>
>>> I'm having some other problems with running it on my own data, but I 
>>> will troubleshoot for some more time to see if I can solve it on my own.
>>>
>>> Thanks,
>>> Daniel
>>>
>>> On Thursday, July 25, 2024 at 9:19:18 AM UTC-4 alist...@gmail.com wrote:
>>>
>>>> Hi both,
>>>>
>>>> @both which version(s) of Warp are you attempting to run? and on which 
>>>> cards?
>>>>
>>>> We recently moved from CUDA 11.7 to 11.8 
>>>> <https://github.com/warpem/warp/pull/184> which bumped the maximum 
>>>> compute capability from 8.6 to 9.0 (useful table 
>>>> <https://stackoverflow.com/questions/28932864/which-compute-capability-is-supported-by-which-cuda-versions/28933055#28933055>
>>>> ).
>>>>
>>>> @Jesse we've run workshops on A10 cards (CC 8.6) without issue so I 
>>>> think there may be something up with your installation.
>>>> The 11GB memory on the 1080Ti is likely to limit you for big 
>>>> refinements indeed, unfortunately those cards are a bit behind the times. 
>>>> You could try --cpu_memory in MCore if forced to used these cards for 
>>>> whatever reason.
>>>>
>>>> Cheers,
>>>>
>>>> Alister
>>>>
>>>>
>>>>
>>>> On Wed, 24 Jul 2024 at 09:52, Jesse Hansen <bluu...@gmail.com> wrote:
>>>>
>>>>> I believe I was getting similar errors when testing on different nodes.
>>>>>
>>>>> for what it's worth, I have found warptools is finicky with which 
>>>>> nodes it will run on in our cluster.  I am still trying to track down the 
>>>>> pattern, but currently it only runs with full functionality on our node 
>>>>> with an NVIDIA GeForce RTX 2080 Ti. 
>>>>>
>>>>> I also tried on a node with Nvidia A10 and it did not run. I ran the 
>>>>> exact same job on the node above and it worked.
>>>>>
>>>>> I tested on GeForce GTX 1080 Ti and it worked okay for warptools but 
>>>>> not mtools.  
>>>>>
>>>>> best,
>>>>>
>>>>> Jesse
>>>>> On Tuesday, July 23, 2024 at 11:40:59 PM UTC+2 dxh...@gmail.com wrote:
>>>>>
>>>>>> As an update, I ran the tutorial verbatim with the tutorial data and 
>>>>>> it still produced the same error. Could this be a problem with the 
>>>>>> installation?
>>>>>>
>>>>>> On Tuesday, July 23, 2024 at 10:47:24 AM UTC-4 Daniel Cai wrote:
>>>>>>
>>>>>>> Hi Everyone,
>>>>>>>
>>>>>>> I've been trying to learn warp following the online tutorial with my 
>>>>>>> own data, but sadly encountered a very early error.
>>>>>>> Warp can't connect to workers on the step with motion correction and 
>>>>>>> averaging of the frames. It's able to find the frames but unable to run. I 
>>>>>>> tried changing between .mrc and .tif frame files and modifying the number 
>>>>>>> of processes/gpu, and running it on a more powerful gpu, but haven't had 
>>>>>>> any luck so far.
>>>>>>> I've copied the error below.
>>>>>>>
>>>>>>> Does anyone have a clue why this might be the case? 
>>>>>>>
>>>>>>> Thanks in advance!
>>>>>>> Daniel
>>>>>>>
>>>>>>> Unhandled exception. System.Exception: Couldn't connect to newly 
>>>>>>> created worker at localhost:36779 for 20 seconds, something must be wrong
>>>>>>>    at Warp.WorkerWrapper..ctor(Int32 deviceID, Boolean silent, 
>>>>>>> Boolean attachDebugger) in /opt/warp/WarpLib/WorkerWrapper.cs:line 104
>>>>>>>    at WarpTools.Commands.DistributedOptions.GetWorkers(Boolean 
>>>>>>> attachDebugger) in /opt/warp/WarpTools/Commands/DistributedOptions.cs:line 
>>>>>>> 57
>>>>>>>    at WarpTools.Commands.MotionCTFFrameseries.Run(Object options) in 
>>>>>>> /opt/warp/WarpTools/Commands/Frameseries/MotionCTFFrameseries.cs:line 187
>>>>>>>    at WarpTools.WarpTools.Run(Object options) in 
>>>>>>> /opt/warp/WarpTools/Program.cs:line 30
>>>>>>>    at Warp.Tools.CommandLineParserHelper.ParseAndRun(String[] args, 
>>>>>>> Func`2 run, Type[] verbs, String appName) in 
>>>>>>> /opt/warp/WarpLib/Tools/CommandLineParserHelper.cs:line 26
>>>>>>>    at WarpTools.WarpTools.Main(String[] args) in 
>>>>>>> /opt/warp/WarpTools/Program.cs:line 17
>>>>>>>    at WarpTools.WarpTools.<Main>(String[] args)
>>>>>>> /usr/local/apps/warp/2.0.0dev11/bin/WarpTools: line 10: 846294 
>>>>>>> Aborted                 singularity run ${nv_opt} "$(dirname $(readlink -f 
>>>>>>> ${BASH_SOURCE[0]}))/warp_2.0.0dev11.sif" "$(basename $0)" "$@"
>>>>>>>

>>>>> .
